project_structure: Monorepo (pnpm workspaces): packages/cli (citty-based CLI; commands/install.ts orchestrates install pipeline, install/skills-and-hooks.ts owns per-client copy+merge primitives, install/hooks-orchestrator.ts re-installs hooks idempotently, install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts mirrors install in reverse), packages/server (MCP server), packages/shared (Zod schemas + i18n + node helpers atomicWriteText/atomicWriteJson). Templates ship under packages/cli/templates/{skills,hooks,bootstrap,agents-md}/. Critical: packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/{CLAUDE.md,codex-AGENTS-header.md,cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc} and packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template are ORPHANED (zero references in packages/cli/src) — current install.ts only writes a minimal AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT inline (install.ts L259-267) and inserts a single managed-section into pre-existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.cursor/rules. There is NO existing per-client bootstrap-file writer — rc.19 will introduce one. Integration tests under packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts pin the current marker contract (fabric:knowledge-base:begin/end) and the 3-target SECTION_TARGETS list.

relevant_files.rationale: Main `fab install` entry point and execution-plan orchestrator. The bootstrap stage case at L1002-L1054 is the EXACT insertion site for the new pipeline: replace step `installResults.push(...await runBestEffort("section", ...))` at L1043 with new `writeFabricAgentsSnapshot()` + three new thin-shell writers (claude/codex/cursor managed-block propagation). Also owns AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT at L259-267 which becomes obsolete (the snapshot replaces it) and `agentsMdAction` flow at L661/L724-732 which currently writes the root AGENTS.md anchor — rc.19 must decouple ROOT AGENTS.md (now a managed thin-shell for Codex) from `.fabric/AGENTS.md` (new snapshot).

key_code.description: Inline minimal markdown for root AGENTS.md. Becomes obsolete — root AGENTS.md is reclassified in rc.19 as the Codex managed-block thin shell (byte copy of .fabric/AGENTS.md [+ project-rules.md]); the default-content constant disappears or moves to .fabric/AGENTS.md template content.

Scaffold-stage AGENTS.md writer (atomicWriteText only when absent, preserved otherwise). Needs split: rc.19 still touches root AGENTS.md but with managed-block semantics (insert/replace block while preserving user content outside markers) instead of created-or-preserved binary.

Bootstrap-stage executor that runs all 11 install-step helpers in sequence and reports per-step results. The new pipeline must add: (a) writeFabricAgentsSnapshot(plan.target) — write .fabric/AGENTS.md from template, (b) writeProjectRulesScaffold(plan.target) — optional .fabric/project-rules.md stub, (c) writeClaudeBootstrapThinShell — CLAUDE.md with `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` import line + managed block, (d) writeCodexBootstrapThinShell — root AGENTS.md managed block = snapshot + project-rules concat, (e) writeCursorBootstrapThinShell — .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc managed block. Replaces L1043 single section writer.

Current `"created" | "preserved"` binary. With managed-block semantics rc.19 needs at least `"created" | "updated" | "preserved"` (or migrate to InstallStepStatus union 'written' | 'skipped' | 'missing-managed').

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration centers on this file: every new bootstrap consolidation behavior (snapshot write, three thin-shells, marker rename to fabric:bootstrap) must be wired into the bootstrap stage here. Without these changes the install pipeline still emits the OLD section-only output.

relevant_files.rationale: Owns the managed-block primitives that rc.19 will refactor. `FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN_MARKER` and `FABRIC_SECTION_END_MARKER` (L235-236) need renaming `fabric:knowledge-base` → `fabric:bootstrap`. `FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX` (L245-246) must update to new marker. `addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection` (L638-705) must be split into `writeFabricAgentsSnapshot` (.fabric/AGENTS.md atomic write — new) + three thin-shell writers per client. `buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection` (L291-304) body content moves into the .fabric/AGENTS.md template (now the single source) and is no longer assembled by JS at install time. `SECTION_TARGETS` (L224) becomes obsolete — replaced by a per-client mapping with three distinct write strategies (Claude=import shell, Codex=byte-copy concat, Cursor=byte-copy concat with frontmatter).

key_code.description: HTML-comment marker strings `<!-- fabric:knowledge-base:begin/end -->`. RENAME to `<!-- fabric:bootstrap:begin/end -->` in rc.19 per locked decision. Mirrors needed in uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts L278 and tests.
Pattern matching the entire managed region. Update marker substring; same shape (non-greedy body, optional preceding blank-line). Reused for in-place replace + uninstall strip.

Currently `["CLAUDE.md", "AGENTS.md", join(".cursor", "rules")]` — fanout list. rc.19 replaces this with three per-client target descriptors {path, writer, contentBuilder}. Note: current targets include legacy `.cursor/rules` (no extension) which is INCONSISTENT with rc.19 spec `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` — migration risk for existing installs.

Builds the section body (heading + 4 bullets + Language interpolation). rc.19 removes this — content moves into packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/.fabric-AGENTS.md.template (new). The fabricLanguage runtime interpolation moves into a template-render step that reads the same fabric-config.json value.

Reads each SECTION_TARGETS file, regex-matches the marker region, in-place replaces OR appends with blank-line separator. The block-management algorithm is REUSABLE — rc.19 keeps this state machine but: (1) renames step from 'section' to 'bootstrap-block', (2) extracts the block content from .fabric/AGENTS.md + .fabric/project-rules.md instead of from buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection, (3) Claude variant ADDITIONALLY emits @.fabric/AGENTS.md import outside the managed block (idempotent line-add).

Reads fabric_language from .fabric/fabric-config.json with match-existing fallback. Still needed by rc.19 — the snapshot template interpolates this value into the Language bullet; the propagated managed blocks inherit it via byte-copy.

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration: every bootstrap-write helper called from install.ts lives here. The marker-rename + three-way split (snapshot + three thin-shells) requires surgical edits to this module's exports.

relevant_files.rationale: Secondary entry to the bootstrap-stage helpers — `installHooks()` (L79-118) re-runs every primitive on demand (callable post-init or from `fab install` re-run paths) and calls `addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection` at L114. Any new snapshot+thin-shell writers added in install.ts MUST be mirrored here for parity, otherwise a `fab install` re-run after a manual delete of .fabric/AGENTS.md would not restore it (regression vs current single-section behavior).

key_code.description: Re-installable orchestrator covering all 9-11 bootstrap-stage steps. L113-114 invoke addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection — replace with the same five new writers added to install.ts (snapshot + three thin-shells + optional project-rules scaffold). Same best-effort discipline (try/catch per step via runStep).

Post-install validator that resolves each registered hook script to its on-disk file. rc.19 should add a parallel validator: assert .fabric/AGENTS.md exists and each thin-shell's managed block has byte-identical content vs snapshot+project-rules (catches drift between rebuilds).

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration: this is the second consumer of the section-writer primitives. Forgetting to update this file would create a behavioral split between fresh install (correct) and re-install (broken).

relevant_files.rationale: Mirror-image of install. `stripFabricKnowledgeBaseSection` (L295-L358) strips the managed section from CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.cursor/rules using FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX. rc.19 needs marker-name update PLUS: (1) strip the new fabric:bootstrap block from each thin-shell, (2) remove .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot file (NEW deletion target — not just block-strip), (3) optionally remove .fabric/project-rules.md if untouched, (4) Claude variant must also remove the `@.fabric/AGENTS.md` import line (managed import-line conventional dedupe). Must remain idempotent (second uninstall is no-op).

key_code.description: Inverse of addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection — regex-strips the managed region and writes back. RENAME marker, expand to handle the three thin-shells with different post-strip outcomes: Claude keeps the @import line stripped too; Codex/Cursor leave the file as-is (empty thin-shell allowed).

Top-level uninstall orchestrator. Step 1 is currently 'section first'; rc.19 needs new Step 1.5 to delete .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot. Order: thin-shell-strip (block) → @import-line strip (claude only) → snapshot file delete → optionally project-rules.md preservation prompt or auto-keep.

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration: the install↔uninstall symmetry contract is a hard constraint. Every new write in rc.19 install requires a matching strip/delete in uninstall, otherwise `fab uninstall && fab install` no longer round-trips.

relevant_files.rationale: ORPHAN template — zero references in packages/cli/src (verified via grep of `templates/bootstrap`, `bootstrap/CLAUDE`, etc.). Only matched in historical `.workflow/.lite-plan/fabric-v1.0-product-launch-2026-04-19/` plan files. Per user's clean-slate preference (no users yet), rc.19 should DELETE this file (and codex-AGENTS-header.md, cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc) and replace with a single new template `packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/.fabric-AGENTS.md.template` (or similar) that becomes the .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot source.

ORPHAN template — zero references in packages/cli/src. Same disposition as bootstrap/CLAUDE.md: delete in rc.19, replace with the new single-source .fabric/AGENTS.md template. Codex's root AGENTS.md becomes a managed-block thin shell (byte copy of .fabric/AGENTS.md + concat .fabric/project-rules.md) per locked rc.19 decisions.

ORPHAN template — zero references in packages/cli/src. Same disposition: delete. Cursor's .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc becomes a managed-block thin shell with frontmatter (alwaysApply: true) wrapping the byte-copy. Frontmatter is OUTSIDE the managed block; the block body is the snapshot+project-rules concat.

ORPHAN template with placeholder syntax `{ projectName }`, `{ frameworkKind }` — currently unused. Could be REPURPOSED as the `.fabric/AGENTS.md` template source (already has the structural body: CORE RULES, knowledge-layer doc, types/maturity/layers descriptions). Decision needed: write a fresh template or adapt this one. Recommended adaptation: this content + the existing buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection's 4 bullets + Language interpolation marker.

key_code.description: Pre-existing 'Fabric Bootstrap Protocol' markdown with CORE RULES section, knowledge layer doc (5 types / 3 maturity / 2 layers), and {projectName}/{frameworkKind} placeholders. Strong candidate for the new .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot template — covers behavior rules + KB section per rc.19 edge expansion.

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration: rc.19 needs a snapshot template; this orphan already documents Fabric Protocol bootstrap with the exact scope (behavior rules + KB descriptions) — promote rather than rewrite.

relevant_files.rationale: Provides `atomicWriteText` (L18) and `atomicWriteJson` (L52) — the canonical write primitives used by every install step (skills-and-hooks.ts:6 imports both). rc.19 snapshot writer + three thin-shell writers MUST use atomicWriteText (tmpfile + rename) to satisfy the existing atomic-write invariant. No changes needed in this file; just hard dependency.

key_code.description: Write text via tmpfile + rename. Required for half-written-file safety on the snapshot and three thin-shells (especially because the thin-shells will be re-read by AI clients immediately after write).

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration: any new install-time file write inherits the atomic-write requirement. Direct fs.writeFileSync is a known anti-pattern in this codebase (see preserved fixture writes vs install writes).

relevant_files.rationale: PINS the current managed-section contract: L457-L539 verifies single begin/end marker pair across the three targets, idempotency, in-place replace on fabric_language flip, and managed-section overwrite of user edits. Every assertion using SECTION_BEGIN/SECTION_END (the rc.19-renamed markers) and the SECTION_TARGETS list (["AGENTS.md", "CLAUDE.md", ".cursor/rules"]) must update to: (a) new `fabric:bootstrap:` marker, (b) per-client target paths (.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc, not .cursor/rules), (c) NEW assertions for .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot existence + byte equality vs template, (d) NEW byte-equality assertions for thin-shell managed-block contents = snapshot+project-rules concat.

key_code.description: Pre-seeds AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/.cursor/rules then asserts single marker pair + heading order. rc.19 rewrite: pre-seed includes .fabric/AGENTS.md absence (snapshot must be created), then asserts (1) .fabric/AGENTS.md exists with template content, (2) each thin-shell exists with managed block = snapshot byte-copy, (3) Claude additionally has @.fabric/AGENTS.md import line.

key_code.symbol: Test 'replaces the section in place when fabric_language changes'
key_code.description: Flips fabric_language in fabric-config.json and re-runs install. rc.19: now flip propagates to .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot, which in turn re-propagates to all three thin-shells. Test must assert the change in all FOUR files (snapshot + 3 thin-shells).

relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration: this is the regression gate for the install pipeline. Any rc.19 change without updating these tests will fail CI on the marker substring.

relevant_files.rationale: Mirror gate for uninstall. Pins the strip-section contract: marker-delimited region removed, file preserved if it had pre-existing content. rc.19 must extend: (1) verify .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot is deleted, (2) verify @.fabric/AGENTS.md import line removed from CLAUDE.md, (3) marker rename propagated. Same idempotency requirement.
relevant_files.topic_relation: install-flow exploration: validates the install↔uninstall round-trip; if rc.19 adds deletes without updating these assertions, CI silently passes a regression.

relevant_files.rationale: Lock-check and pre-init guard integration test. Touches the fresh-init AGENTS.md write path. Likely needs minor updates if rc.19 changes the root AGENTS.md write semantics from 'created or preserved' (current) to 'managed-block insert' (new).
Verifies atomic-write semantics on init paths (no half-written files on crash). New snapshot write + three thin-shell writes need parallel atomic assertions if the existing test pattern is followed.

Listed separately because it's a SHARED helper between scaffold stage (writes fabric-config.json with detected language at install.ts L408+) and bootstrap stage (reads back the value to interpolate into the section body). rc.19 must preserve the read-after-write ordering: writeDefaultFabricConfig (L400+) runs in scaffold stage; readFabricLanguagePreference runs in bootstrap stage AFTER scaffold completes — same ordering as current install.ts L1042.

Templates directory housing the orphan AGENTS.md.template. Decision needed: keep this dir as the new snapshot template home, or relocate to packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ (alongside the deleted thin-shell templates). Recommended: relocate ALL bootstrap-related templates under packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ with clear filenames (e.g. fabric-agents.md.template, project-rules.md.template) for discoverability — matches the existing skills/ / hooks/ subdir convention.

patterns: Install pipeline patterns observed: (1) Idempotent copy via copyTextIdempotent (skills-and-hooks.ts L711-L729) — read existing, byte-compare, skip if equal, else atomicWriteText; returns InstallStepResult with status='written'|'skipped'. (2) Managed-block insert/replace via FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX state machine (L665-L685) — match region, slice before/after, strip leading newline, reconcat with builder output; ABSENT branch appends with blank-line separator. (3) Best-effort per-step error tolerance (commands/install.ts L1012-L1051 runBestEffort wrapper) — single helper failure doesn't abort bootstrap stage. (4) Template path resolution via findTemplatePath (skills-and-hooks.ts L793-L807) — walks up from import.meta.url until templates/<rel> sibling found; works in both dev (src/) and bundled (dist/) layouts. (5) Per-client fanout via DESTINATIONS const arrays (L99-L158) — single source of truth shared between install and uninstall. (6) Shared markers + regex re-exported across install/uninstall pair to guarantee byte-identical match strings (L235-246). All of these patterns CARRY OVER unchanged into rc.19; the marker string is the only literal that changes.
dependencies: Internal: install.ts → skills-and-hooks.ts (12 named imports L23-L37); install.ts → hooks-orchestrator.ts (installHooks); skills-and-hooks.ts → @fenglimg/fabric-shared/node/atomic-write (atomicWriteText + atomicWriteJson); skills-and-hooks.ts → ../config/json.js (deepMerge for hook configs only — not for bootstrap blocks); hooks-orchestrator.ts → skills-and-hooks.ts (11 named imports including addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection + readFabricLanguagePreference); uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts → skills-and-hooks.ts (re-imports FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX, FABRIC_SECTION_BEGIN_MARKER, FABRIC_SECTION_END_MARKER, SECTION_TARGETS). External: node:fs (existsSync, readFileSync, chmodSync, readdirSync), node:fs/promises (mkdir, readFile), node:path (join, dirname, resolve, parse), node:url (fileURLToPath for template path walking). Templates: packages/cli/templates/{skills,hooks,bootstrap,agents-md}/ — currently 4 subdirs, 2 of which (bootstrap/, agents-md/) are dead code that rc.19 reorganizes. NEW dependency for rc.19: a template-render utility OR plain readFile + interpolate `${fabricLanguage}` substitution (no Handlebars/Mustache — keep deps zero).
integration_points: EXACT insertion sites (file:line precision): (1) packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts L1042-L1043 — replace `const fabricLanguage = readFabricLanguagePreference(plan.target); installResults.push(...await runBestEffort("section", () => addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection(plan.target, fabricLanguage)));` with five new steps: writeFabricAgentsSnapshot, writeProjectRulesScaffold (optional), writeClaudeBootstrapThinShell, writeCodexBootstrapThinShell, writeCursorBootstrapThinShell. (2) packages/cli/src/install/hooks-orchestrator.ts L113-L114 — same five-step replacement (mirror parity). (3) packages/cli/src/install/skills-and-hooks.ts L235-L236 — rename marker constants (`fabric:knowledge-base` → `fabric:bootstrap`); L245-L246 — update FABRIC_SECTION_REGEX literal accordingly; L224 — replace SECTION_TARGETS list with per-client target-descriptor map; L291-L304 — DELETE buildFabricKnowledgeBaseSection (content migrates to template file); L638-L705 — split addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection into 4 new exports (snapshot writer + 3 thin-shell writers). (4) packages/cli/src/install/uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts L278 — update marker docstring; L295-L358 stripFabricKnowledgeBaseSection — extend to handle 3 thin-shells distinct paths + .fabric/AGENTS.md file delete + @import-line strip on Claude. (5) packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts L259-L267 — DELETE AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT (replaced by .fabric/AGENTS.md template + Codex thin-shell copy logic). (6) packages/cli/src/commands/install.ts L656-L661 + L724-L732 — REVIEW agentsMdAction flow: with rc.19 the root AGENTS.md is now managed by the Codex thin-shell writer in the BOOTSTRAP stage, not the SCAFFOLD stage; remove the scaffold-stage write to avoid double-write race. (7) NEW FILE packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/fabric-agents.md.template (or similar) — content source for .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot. (8) NEW FILE (optional template) packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/project-rules.md.template — initial content for .fabric/project-rules.md (or skip scaffold; document the convention only). (9) packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L457-L539 — rewrite Fabric Knowledge Base section tests as 'fabric:bootstrap snapshot + thin-shells' tests with the four new write targets.
constraints: (1) ATOMIC WRITE INVARIANT — all new bootstrap-stage writes (snapshot + 3 thin-shells + optional project-rules) MUST go through atomicWriteText (already imported in skills-and-hooks.ts L6). Direct writeFileSync is forbidden in install paths (verified by existing test_target init-atomic.test.ts). (2) IDEMPOTENCY — repeated `fab install` must produce byte-identical output (snapshot tree equality already tested at install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts L221-L237 across .claude/.codex/.cursor). New writes must hash-equal on re-run; managed-block in-place replace algorithm from current addFabricKnowledgeBaseSection is the proven template. (3) BEST-EFFORT — each new step wrapped in runBestEffort (install.ts) / runStep (hooks-orchestrator.ts) — single client failure must not abort. (4) PRESERVE USER CONTENT OUTSIDE MARKERS — current managed-section convention (overwrite inside markers, byte-preserve outside) carries over to all three thin-shells. Claude's @import line is a SEPARATE managed line (idempotent line-add OUTSIDE the block) per locked decision. (5) MARKER RENAME IS A BREAKING CHANGE — old `fabric:knowledge-base` markers in user repos will not match the new regex; rc.19 must EITHER (a) ship a compat regex that matches both old + new markers then writes only new on update, OR (b) document that uninstall must be run on the old marker version first. Per user's clean-slate preference (zero users) option (b) is acceptable. (6) FABRIC_LANGUAGE INTERPOLATION — the snapshot template must contain a substitution token (e.g. `{{ fabric_language }}` or `${FABRIC_LANGUAGE}`) replaced at install time from readFabricLanguagePreference. Choose a token style that does not collide with markdown content; ${TOKEN} with shell-style substitution is safer than {{}} (no Handlebars dep). (7) TEMPLATE PATH RESOLUTION — findTemplatePath at skills-and-hooks.ts L793-L807 already handles dev + bundled layouts; new templates under packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ inherit this for free. (8) UNINSTALL SYMMETRY — every new write needs a matching strip/delete in uninstall-skills-and-hooks.ts to preserve `uninstall && install` round-trip. (9) TEST SNAPSHOT UPDATES — cli-surface.test.ts.snap likely unchanged (no new CLI args), but install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts and uninstall-skills-and-hooks.test.ts need substantial rewrites; init-atomic.test.ts likely needs new atomic-write assertions for the snapshot file. (10) i18n — current bootstrap-stage messages reference 'section' step (cli.install.stages.bootstrap, formatInitStageResult); new step names ('bootstrap-snapshot', 'bootstrap-block-claude', etc.) need locale entries in packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/{en.ts,zh-CN.ts}. (11) NO --dry-run MODE EXISTS YET FOR BOOTSTRAP STAGE — install.ts has `--dry-run` flag (L289-L293) but only the scaffold-stage diff classification uses it (DiffFileState at L75-L79); bootstrap stage runs fully regardless. rc.19 may need to extend diff classification to .fabric/AGENTS.md and the three thin-shells if dry-run parity is required (defer or scope to a follow-up).

clarification_needs.question: Where should the new `.fabric/AGENTS.md` snapshot template ship under packages/cli/templates/?
clarification_needs.context: Current templates/ has skills/, hooks/, bootstrap/, agents-md/. The bootstrap/ and agents-md/ subdirs are both orphaned by current code. Three reasonable placements: (a) packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/fabric-agents.md.template — reclaim the existing bootstrap/ dir (delete its current 3 dead files first), (b) packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template — adapt the existing orphan file in-place, (c) packages/cli/templates/.fabric/AGENTS.md.template — mirror the on-disk destination layout for discoverability. Option (a) groups bootstrap-related assets together and follows the skills/ / hooks/ convention; recommended.
options: Reclaim packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ — delete the 3 orphan files (CLAUDE.md, codex-AGENTS-header.md, cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc) and add fabric-agents.md.template + optional project-rules.md.template
Adapt packages/cli/templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template in place — content already aligns with rc.19 scope

clarification_needs.question: Should `.fabric/project-rules.md` be scaffolded at install time, or left to the user to create on demand?
clarification_needs.context: Locked rc.19 spec says project customs go to .fabric/project-rules.md, and thin-shell managed blocks concat snapshot + `\n---\n` + project-rules.md IF EXISTS. Two install behaviors: (a) scaffold an empty (or minimal stub) project-rules.md at install time so users discover the file; thin-shells then always include the divider + body even if body is empty, (b) do NOT scaffold; install only writes the snapshot, thin-shells omit divider when project-rules absent. Option (b) is more idempotent (no scaffold-or-preserve binary) but less discoverable.
options: Scaffold a stub .fabric/project-rules.md at install if absent (idempotent-preserved on re-run, like AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT today)
Do not scaffold — thin-shells conditionally include project-rules content only when file exists; users opt in by creating the file manually
Scaffold but write only an HTML comment placeholder (`<!-- Add project-specific rules here -->`) so the file's presence is visible in git status without noise

clarification_needs.question: How should the marker rename `fabric:knowledge-base` → `fabric:bootstrap` handle existing installs?
clarification_needs.context: Per user's locked clean-slate preference (zero users on Fabric), backward compatibility for the old marker is not required. Options: (a) hard rename — new regex matches only the new marker; existing installs with old marker will get a SECOND managed block appended (regression!), (b) compat regex matches BOTH markers; install writes new marker only; uninstall strips both — safest path even with zero users, costs ~5 LOC, (c) require `fab uninstall` before upgrading from any pre-rc.19 install. Given the user's broader preference for clean-slate refactors but the trivial cost of (b), recommend (b) as a defensive cheap insurance.
options: Hard rename — match only `fabric:bootstrap`; document upgrade-from-pre-rc.19 requires `fab uninstall && fab install`
Compat regex matches BOTH `fabric:knowledge-base` and `fabric:bootstrap`; install always writes the new marker; uninstall strips both
Migration helper — `fab doctor --fix` rewrites old marker to new marker in place

clarification_needs.question: Should the root AGENTS.md write move entirely from scaffold-stage to bootstrap-stage?
clarification_needs.context: Current install.ts splits AGENTS.md ownership: scaffold-stage writes AGENTS_MD_DEFAULT_CONTENT when absent (L730-L732); bootstrap-stage inserts the fabric:knowledge-base section (L1043). rc.19 reclassifies root AGENTS.md as the Codex thin-shell (byte-copy of snapshot + project-rules within managed block). Two designs: (a) keep scaffold-stage write for the OUTSIDE-markers body, bootstrap-stage handles managed block — preserves current two-stage split, (b) move ALL AGENTS.md ownership into bootstrap-stage — scaffold no longer touches AGENTS.md, simpler single-writer model. Option (b) is cleaner (one stage owns one file) and matches the new thin-shell semantics where the WHOLE FILE is essentially fabric-managed except for user additions outside markers.
options: Keep current split — scaffold writes the file shell, bootstrap inserts/updates the managed block
Move all AGENTS.md ownership to bootstrap-stage — scaffold-stage no longer touches AGENTS.md
Move to bootstrap, additionally write the thin-shell's @-import / managed-block structure as the ENTIRE file content on fresh install (no separate 'default content' for the outside-markers area)

clarification_needs.question: Path for the Cursor thin-shell — `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc` or migrate the existing `.cursor/rules` (no extension) target?
clarification_needs.context: Current install writes the managed section into a path literally named `.cursor/rules` (no .mdc extension, no subdirectory) — see SECTION_TARGETS at skills-and-hooks.ts L224 `join(".cursor", "rules")`. Per Cursor docs, the convention is `.cursor/rules/<name>.mdc` (rules is a DIRECTORY of .mdc files). The current code is inconsistent with Cursor's actual convention. rc.19 locked decision says `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc`. Need to: (a) treat as bug fix — write new file, (b) also leave a deprecation strip in uninstall for the old `.cursor/rules` flat-file path so users who installed pre-rc.19 don't keep cruft.
options: Just write the new path `.cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc`; ignore any pre-existing `.cursor/rules` flat file (let uninstall handle)
Write new path AND strip any pre-existing `.cursor/rules` flat file containing the old marker (defensive migration in install)
Write new path; extend uninstall to clean up old `.cursor/rules` flat file with old marker; install does NOT touch the old path

clarification_needs.question: Should the rc.19 install pipeline support a `--clients` flag to install only specific client thin-shells (e.g. Claude-only)?
clarification_needs.context: Current install writes section to ALL THREE targets unconditionally (if pre-existing). No per-client install scoping. Codebase memory notes 'Fabric supported clients scope — only Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI; dropped Windsurf/Roo/Gemini' — suggests the three-client scope is stable. Per user's CLI design principle '能交互选的就别做 flag' (let users pick interactively rather than flags), this should NOT be a flag. But: do we still write ALL three thin-shells unconditionally, or skip a client when its tooling artifacts are absent (e.g. no .codex/ dir means no Codex installed)? Current code writes section unconditionally if marker file exists. rc.19 expansion to all three thin-shells should write them all (snapshot ownership = the three thin-shells are derived artifacts, not optional).
options: Write all three thin-shells unconditionally on every install (current behavior generalized)
Skip a client's thin-shell when its directory artifacts (.claude/, .codex/, .cursor/) are absent — auto-detect, no flag
Add a `--clients` flag to install command for per-client scoping (violates user's CLI design principle)

_metadata.task_description: rc.19 bootstrap consolidation install-flow exploration — collapse three end-client bootstrap templates into single source .fabric/AGENTS.md; refactor `fab install` to write the snapshot and propagate to three ends via thin-shell + managed block.
